profile.name: David Drake
profile.bio: Engineering leader · Narrative compression researcher · Author of I Will Not Drink With You Today · Theatre → CS → the space between

repos.name: data.gov
repos.description: Data.gov source code and issue tracker

repos.name: hots-cheat-sheet
repos.description: Heroes of the Storm Cheat Sheet extracts all of the Heroes of the Storm builds from Icy Veins and puts them into CSV to create a cheat sheet.

repos.name: human-headers
repos.description: Human Headers is a project to let developers put a little more of themselves into their work without visibly impacting the web.

repos.name: intro.js
repos.description: A better way for new feature introduction and step-by-step users guide for your website and project.

repos.name: jenks
repos.description: PHP Implementation of Jenks Natural Breaks Optimization for Choropleth Mapping

repos.name: journal-down
repos.description: Journal in Markdown, using MacDown, backed up to your iCloud.

repos.name: nasa-apod-desktop
repos.description: Automatically downloads and sets the NASA picture of the day (APOD) as your desktop in Ubuntu.

repos.name: react-flow-org-chart
repos.description: Proof of concept of an org chart generator using React Flow.

repos.name: swagger-ui
repos.description: Swagger UI is a collection of HTML, Javascript, and CSS assets that dynamically generate beautiful documentation from a Swagger-compliant API.

repos.name: Third-Party
repos.description: Third Party is an open source app to easily create accountability for yourself or others.

commits.message: feat: build complete RedDep site with About page and NDA trust messaging
commits.full_message:
feat: build complete RedDep site with About page and NDA trust messaging

Implements the full single-page marketing and booking site from the
initial Next.js scaffold, plus a dedicated /about page with expanded
leader profile, capability areas, and NDA trust banner.

Key additions:
- Pride-themed brand system (colors, typography, gradient bar)
- All home page sections (Hero, Mission/Vision, Services, Values, About, Booking, Contact)
- Stripe checkout integration with Cal.com booking flow
- /about page with LeaderProfile, CapabilityAreas, NdaTrustBanner
- Pathname-aware header navigation (anchor links on home, full paths elsewhere)
- NDA/confidentiality messaging in Contact section
- SEO metadata, sitemap, robots.txt, JSON-LD structured data
- WCAG AA accessibility (skip nav, focus indicators, reduced motion, semantic HTML)
- Vercel deployment config with security headers
commits.author: David Drake

commits.message: 🌌✨🏳️‍🌈 the space between — full profile README with real-deal Hubble imagery!! 🏳️‍⚧️✨🔭
commits.full_message:
🌌✨🏳️‍🌈 the space between — full profile README with real-deal Hubble imagery!! 🏳️‍⚧️✨🔭

swapped out the AI-generated dragon banner for an actual NASA Hubble Space
Telescope photo from Unsplash — because why use fake cosmos when the real
universe is RIGHT THERE being gorgeous?? 💜💙🩷

built out the whole profile README with:
  ✨ intro that captures the theatre→CS→AI arc
  📖 the book, the writing, the whole story
  🔭 what's current (narrative compression! AI! substack!)
  ⭐ notable open source (nasa-apod-desktop still slapping at 151 stars)
  🧰 tech stack
  🌲 personal flavor (commodore 64 tattoo!! oregon forever!!)
  🔗 all the socials and links

this is a human being's profile, not a template. it breathes. 🫁🌈

issue.title: Add Wayland support for wallpaper setting
issue.body:
## Problem
The current wallpaper setting function doesn't work properly on Ubuntu 22.04 with Wayland sessions because `gsettings` behaves differently in Wayland vs X11. Also the code is change to work in python3.

## Solution
- Added automatic detection of Wayland sessions using environment variables
- For Wayland sessions, set both `picture-uri` and `picture-uri-dark` properties
- Maintains full backward compatibility with X11 sessions
- No breaking changes to existing functionality

## Testing
Tested on:
- Ubuntu 22.04 with Wayland (fixed the issue)
- Should work on X11 sessions (backward compatible)

## Changes
- Modified `set_gnome_wallpaper()` function to detect session type
- Added dual property setting for Wayland compatibility

comment.body:
Hey @LorenzoTell — thank you for this substantial contribution. There's clearly a lot of work here, and several parts of it are genuinely valuable.

The Python 3 migration work you did (urllib, subprocess, print functions, LANCZOS, etc.) is solid, but as it turns out, the codebase has already been independently ported to Python 3 on our end. The Wayland wallpaper fix — setting both `picture-uri` and `picture-uri-dark` — is a great catch for GNOME on Wayland, and I'll be incorporating that idea.

You also found several real bugs that I want to call out and give you credit for:
- The missing `return` in `human_readable_size()` for GB-range values
- The `largest` variable never being updated in `find_resolution()`
- The missing default for the `content-length` header in `get_image_info()`

I'll be cherry-picking those fixes into the current codebase. I'm closing the PR because the full diff changes too many things at once (config defaults, removal of the `main()` wrapper, dropping `lxml`, etc.) to merge cleanly, but your bug-finding and Wayland work are appreciated and won't go to waste. Thanks for the effort.

issue.title: Bump lxml from 2.3.6 to 4.9.1

labels.name: dependencies
labels.description: Pull requests that update a dependency file

issue.body:
Bumps [lxml](https://github.com/lxml/lxml) from 2.3.6 to 4.9.1.
Changelog

Sourced from lxml's changelog.

4.9.1 (2022-07-01)

Bugs fixed

  • A crash was resolved when using iterwalk() (or canonicalize()) after parsing certain incorrect input. Note that iterwalk() can crash on valid input parsed with the same parser after failing to parse the incorrect input.

4.9.0 (2022-06-01)

Bugs fixed

  • GH#341: The mixin inheritance order in lxml.html was corrected. Patch by xmo-odoo.

Other changes

  • Built with Cython 0.29.30 to adapt to changes in Python 3.11 and 3.12.

  • Wheels include zlib 1.2.12, libxml2 2.9.14 and libxslt 1.1.35 (libxml2 2.9.12+ and libxslt 1.1.34 on Windows).

  • GH#343: Windows-AArch64 build support in Visual Studio. Patch by Steve Dower.

4.8.0 (2022-02-17)

Features added

  • GH#337: Path-like objects are now supported throughout the API instead of just strings. Patch by Henning Janssen.

  • The ElementMaker now supports QName values as tags, which always override the default namespace of the factory.

Bugs fixed

  • GH#338: In lxml.objectify, the XSI float annotation "nan" and "inf" were spelled in lower case, whereas XML Schema datatypes define them as "NaN" and "INF" respectively.

... (truncated)

Commits
  • d01872c Prevent parse failure in new test from leaking into later test runs.
  • d65e632 Prepare release of lxml 4.9.1.
  • 86368e9 Fix a crash when incorrect parser input occurs together with usages of iterwa...
  • 50c2764 Delete unused Travis CI config and reference in docs (GH-345)
  • 8f0bf2d Try to speed up the musllinux AArch64 build by splitting the different CPytho...
  • b9f7074 Remove debug print from test.
  • b224e0f Try to install 'xz' in wheel builds, if available, since it's now needed to e...
  • 897ebfa Update macOS deployment target version from 10.14 to 10.15 since 10.14 starts...
  • 853c9e9 Prepare release of 4.9.0.
  • d3f77e6 Add a test for https://bugs.launchpad.net/lxml/+bug/1965070 leaving out the a...
  • Additional commits viewable in compare view

[![Dependabot compatibility score](https://dependabot-badges.githubapp.com/badges/compatibility_score?dependency-name=lxml&package-manager=pip&previous-version=2.3.6&new-version=4.9.1)](https://docs.github.com/en/github/managing-security-vulnerabilities/about-dependabot-security-updates#about-compatibility-scores) Dependabot will resolve any conflicts with this PR as long as you don't alter it yourself. You can also trigger a rebase manually by commenting `@dependabot rebase`. [//]: # (dependabot-automerge-start) [//]: # (dependabot-automerge-end) ---
Dependabot commands and options
You can trigger Dependabot actions by commenting on this PR: - `@dependabot rebase` will rebase this PR - `@dependabot recreate` will recreate this PR, overwriting any edits that have been made to it - `@dependabot merge` will merge this PR after your CI passes on it - `@dependabot squash and merge` will squash and merge this PR after your CI passes on it - `@dependabot cancel merge` will cancel a previously requested merge and block automerging - `@dependabot reopen` will reopen this PR if it is closed - `@dependabot close` will close this PR and stop Dependabot recreating it. You can achieve the same result by closing it manually - `@dependabot ignore this major version` will close this PR and stop Dependabot creating any more for this major version (unless you reopen the PR or upgrade to it yourself) - `@dependabot ignore this minor version` will close this PR and stop Dependabot creating any more for this minor version (unless you reopen the PR or upgrade to it yourself) - `@dependabot ignore this dependency` will close this PR and stop Dependabot creating any more for this dependency (unless you reopen the PR or upgrade to it yourself) - `@dependabot use these labels` will set the current labels as the default for future PRs for this repo and language - `@dependabot use these reviewers` will set the current reviewers as the default for future PRs for this repo and language - `@dependabot use these assignees` will set the current assignees as the default for future PRs for this repo and language - `@dependabot use this milestone` will set the current milestone as the default for future PRs for this repo and language You can disable automated security fix PRs for this repo from the [Security Alerts page](https://github.com/randomdrake/nasa-apod-desktop/network/alerts).

comment.body: Closing this Dependabot PR — thanks for the automated security nudge, but it's been overtaken by events. The project's dependencies have been modernized to use `lxml>=5.0` and `Pillow>=10.0` (replacing the old `PIL==1.1.7`), so the bump to 4.9.1 proposed here is no longer needed. Appreciate Dependabot keeping an eye on things.

issue.title: Pip install issues
issue.body:
My environment: Debian 8
Various switches on pip, more apt-get packages, and tricks for virtualenv were needed to complete install.

comment.body:
Hey @jharmn — thanks for documenting the installation pain points you ran into on Debian 8. Dependency installation has historically been one of the trickiest parts of getting this script running, and you took the time to write up what worked for you, which is appreciated.

Since 2015, the landscape has shifted considerably: the `PIL` package has been fully replaced by `Pillow`, the `--allow-external` and `--allow-unverified` pip flags have been removed, and the apt packages you referenced (`python-imaging`) no longer exist in modern distributions. The project's dependencies have also been modernized to use `Pillow>=10.0` and `lxml>=5.0`.

I'm closing this out since the content is no longer applicable to current systems, but thanks for contributing back your findings at the time.

issue.title: Mac OSX Support
issue.body: Adding code to support wallpaper changing on Mac OSX

comment.body:
Hey @simonrw — thank you for working on macOS support! Having NASA's Astronomy Picture of the Day as your wallpaper on a Mac is a great idea, and you were the first to take a shot at making it happen.

Since 2012, both the codebase and macOS itself have changed dramatically. The script has been rewritten for Python 3, and modern macOS versions (Ventura and later) have moved away from the System Preferences AppleScript model that this PR targets. The resolution detection (`xrandr`) and wallpaper setting (`gsettings`) are also Linux-specific, so proper macOS support would need its own detection paths throughout.

I'm closing this PR, but macOS support remains a genuinely desirable feature. If it gets built in the future, it'll need a fresh implementation targeting modern macOS APIs and Python 3 — but your PR proved the concept that it's possible. Thanks for the contribution.

issue.title: Added functionality to differentiate between Window Managers
issue.body: Hello. Neat little script. I've added about five lines of code that now make sure that it can run on both Gnome and xfce. It is hard, if not impossible, to do this in the general case so I just have it read the output of 'top' and search for specific processes ('gnome-sessions' for gnome, 'xfwm4' for xfce4).

comment.body:
Hey @xraymemory — thanks for taking a crack at adding multi-window-manager support. The goal of detecting whether the user is running GNOME vs. XFCE and setting the wallpaper accordingly was a worthwhile one.

As @tgulacsi noted at the time, parsing `top` output for process detection is fragile and there are better approaches (like checking environment variables or using `xprop`). The PR also inadvertently includes some debugging artifacts. Since then, the codebase has been rewritten for Python 3, so this can no longer be applied. Closing this out — but thanks for the contribution and the early exploration of multi-DE support.

issue.title: Download path, window geometry discovery, plus wallpaper setting on XFCE4
issue.body:
1. Add flags for command-line overriding of download path, image size and SHOW_DEBUG.
2. get default download path from glib, if importable - otherwise use $HOME/Downloads
3. get default image size using xprop (I think it exists on every X11)
4. format flake8-compatible.

comment.body:
Hey @tgulacsi — thank you for this really thoughtful and ambitious contribution. You were ahead of your time on several fronts here: auto-detecting resolution, auto-detecting the download folder, adding XFCE4 support, and introducing command-line arguments. That's a lot of good ideas packed into one PR.

Since this was opened, the codebase has been significantly modernized — it's now Python 3 and already has auto-resolution detection (via `xrandr`) and auto-download-path detection (via `GLib`), covering two of the big features you proposed here, just implemented differently. The Python 2 imports (`optparse`, old `glib`, `urllib2`) and structure mean this can't be merged into the current code.

I'm closing this out, but wanted to acknowledge that features like CLI argument support and multi-desktop-environment support are still interesting ideas for the future. If they get built, your PR laid the groundwork for thinking about them. Thanks for the effort you put into this.

issue.title: Keep the aspect ratio through ImageOps.fit
issue.body:
With the use of ImageOps.fit the aspect ratio of the picture isn't distorted. This cuts off (in a pretty dumb way) that is outside of the center.
A better approach might be something based on image entropy to cut out "uninteresting" parts of the images. Reddit uses a setup similar to this to make good thumbnails.

comment.body:
Hey @milot-mirdita — thank you for this contribution, and apologies for letting it sit so long without a response. The idea of using `ImageOps.fit()` to preserve aspect ratio instead of stretching is a genuinely good one, and your observation about the distortion problem was spot-on.

Since this was opened, the codebase has gone through a full Python 3 rewrite, and `Image.ANTIALIAS` (used in the PR) has been removed from modern Pillow in favor of `Image.LANCZOS`. Between the merge conflicts and the API changes, this PR can't be applied as-is anymore. I'm going to close it, but the core idea is worth revisiting — if aspect-ratio-aware resizing gets added down the road, your PR was the original inspiration. Thanks again for taking the time.
